content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — privacy policy states "We do not collect, process, or store your location data" but the app uses CoreLocation with `startUpdatingLocation()` and stores a route array (App/RunSessionManager.swift:9-16), and the description advertises GPS run mapping (description.txt:1).
Pierre: Apple flags direct contradictions between a privacy policy and actual data collection under 5.1.1(i), and this is a clear one — the app's core feature is GPS route tracking, yet the policy explicitly denies collecting location. Update the fetched policy at https://runtracker.example/privacy to disclose location collection (even if only stored on-device), and ensure the PrivacyInfo manifest and App Privacy narrative also declare precise location.
